expectations.evidence: Axes: 'whether the current sellout is the new normal or a fading news bump, and whether your co-teacher is actually still here.' The lease choice is scored as a strategy in Q9, never branched on.
expectations.text: axes-are-external-unknowns: branching happens on demand durability and the co-teacher's move — never on the lease decision itself

expectations.evidence: Five elicitations with stated assumptions, including 'Do you have cash reserve to cover the added rent for several months if bookings cool off?' and the partnership-agreement question.
expectations.text: elicits-missing-facts: rent, savings, or comparable financials appear as questions to the asker inside the response

expectations.evidence: 'Your fall session's sell-through rate' as the demand watchpoint; 'ask her directly this week' converting the co-teacher unknown into a checkable conversation.
expectations.text: watchpoint-observable: at least one watchpoint names a checkable event

expectations.evidence: Q1 through Q9 all surfaced. Q4 caught the correlation trap live: 'if your co-teacher is a big part of why classes sell out... these two axes collapse into one' — the exact correlated-axes failure the examples warn about, checked honestly.
expectations.text: process-visible: chain questions appear in the response before their answers

expectations.evidence: Narrative close, then: 'say yes to the space, but not to the lease exactly as handed to you' — break clause before Friday, flexible fit-out, partnership agreement this week. Weakest tile named (paid for an unused exit ramp). Recommendation is the final element.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-and-committed: the reply ends on a committed move, never a bare 'it depends'
